title: Property Manager Assistant (Virtual)
description:
Job Title:

Property Manager Assistant (Virtual)



About Us:

We are real estate investors and property managers based in the United States. We manage rental properties including single-family homes and multi-unit buildings. We are looking for an experienced, detail-oriented Virtual Assistant to join our team long-term.



Role Overview:

You will help manage day-to-day property operations, coordinate with tenants and vendors, track payments, prepare notices, market properties for rent, assist with lease-ups, and support research projects for acquisitions and due diligence, among other responsibilities.



Key Responsibilities:



Respond to tenant inquiries via
, text, and phone during business hours (Eastern Time)



Schedule showings and coordinate with leasing agents or TaskRabbit runners for on-site tasks



Monitor rent payments in Rentec Direct and prepare notices for delivery (late notices, compliance notices, rent demands, eviction notices)



Track maintenance requests and coordinate with vendors



Prepare and send lease agreements, renewals, and related documents



Assist with marketing vacant units (listing ads, updating online platforms, field inquiries)



Conduct property research as part of due diligence assistance, including:



Pulling tax assessor cards



Downloading GIS maps



Researching zoning details and ordinances



Collecting public records and property data for analysis



Maintain accurate records in our property management systems and spreadsheets



Requirements:



At least 3 years of experience as a Property Management Virtual Assistant



Experience using Rentec Direct or similar property management software



Strong English communication skills (written and spoken, neutral accent preferred)



Professional phone manner and ability to follow scripts when needed



Solid experience handling property research and due diligence tasks



Highly organized, self-directed, and detail-focused



Reliable high-speed internet and backup power preferred



Work Hours:



Full-time: 40 hours/week



Must be available during US business hours 8-5pm (Eastern Time)



Compensation:



Starting Rate: $5–6/hour (depending on experience)



Bonuses: Performance-based incentives



Other Benefits: Paid holidays and paid time off after 6 months



How to Apply:



Send your updated resume.



Record a 1–2 minute audio introduction explaining your experience in property management and due diligence tasks.



Answer these questions:



What property management software have you used, and for how long?



Can you describe your experience pulling tax cards, GIS maps, and zoning info?



What steps do you take to stay organized when managing many properties and projects?



What are your internet speed and your backup plans if there is a power outage?



What US-based companies have you worked for?  Can we call them as a work reference?

title: Real Estate Virtual Assistant (Due Diligence, Research & Analysis) – Part-Time (15 hrs/wk, Growth to Full-Time)
description:
We are seeking a highly detail-oriented and analytical Real Estate Virtual Assistant with a bachelor’s degree in a related field (real estate, business, finance, urban planning, or similar) to assist with property due diligence, research, and financial analysis.



This role starts at 15 hours per week with set hours but has the potential to increase to full-time based on performance.

Responsibilities:

Land & Property Due Diligence & Analysis:

- Call city/town offices, registrars of deeds, and local departments to verify property details.

- Compile tax cards, deeds, property ownership records, and title history.

- Confirm property boundaries, access to roads, and landlocked status.

- Check for wetlands, zoning restrictions, and land use regulations.

- Verify existing utilities (water, sewer, electric, gas, etc.) on-site.

- Analyze property slope and determine feasibility for building.

- Investigate permits pulled, surveys, and plot plans.

House & Rental Property Research & Financial Analysis:

- Confirm permits, property age, and historical data.

- Review Google Maps images to assess last known property condition.

- Conduct comparative market analysis (comps) for property valuation.

- Gather average rental rates for investment properties.

- Input financial data into spreadsheets to underwrite and analyze deals.

- Draft and prepare Purchase & Sales Agreements.

- Cross-check expense data and utility costs for properties.

Qualifications & Requirements:

? Bachelor’s degree in a related field (Real Estate, Business, Finance, Urban Planning, or similar).

? Fluent in English (Written & Spoken) – Must be comfortable making phone calls and communicating professionally with U.S. city and town offices.

? Experience in real estate research, due diligence, and financial analysis.

? Familiarity with real estate documents (tax cards, deeds, property surveys, etc.).

? Strong internet research skills and ability to make calls to U.S. city/town offices.

? Proficiency in Google Sheets/Excel for underwriting and financial modeling.

? Ability to work independently and meet deadlines efficiently.

? Excellent written and verbal English communication skills.

Preferred (Not Required):

- Background in real estate wholesaling, investing, or property management.

- Experience using Zillow, Redfin, PropStream, LandGlide, or similar tools.

Position Details:

? Part-Time (15 Hours/Week)

? Schedule: Monday, Wednesday, Friday – 9 AM - 2 PM EST (Must be available during these hours)

? Pay Rate: $7/hour

? Growth Opportunity: More hours and potential full-time role based on performance

How to Apply:

To apply, please submit the following:

1. Resume highlighting relevant experience.

2. Cover Letter explaining why you are the right fit for this role.

3. Voice Sample (Required):

  - A short recording explaining why you are a great fit for this role.

  - If available, a sample of you making a professional call (e.g., speaking with a government office, customer service, or real estate inquiry).

  - Applications **will not be considered** without at least the recording sample explaining why you are a good fit.

title: Inbound Sales Closer – Phone Sales (High Commission)
description:

This is a closing role, not appointment setting.



All leads are inbound. Homeowners contact us first because they are interested in selling their property.



Your job is to speak with the homeowner, understand their situation, negotiate the price, and secure a signed agreement.



If you are confident on the phone and enjoy closing deals, this role has strong earning potential.



Responsibilities



Call inbound leads quickly

Build rapport with homeowners

Understand the seller’s situation

Negotiate pricing and terms

Close agreements over the phone

Follow up with warm leads

Keep accurate notes in the CRM



This role focuses on closing deals, not scheduling appointments.



Requirements



Strong spoken English

Confident on the phone

Sales closing experience preferred

Comfortable handling objections

Competitive and results-driven

Reliable internet and headset



Experience That Helps



Phone sales

High-ticket sales

Inbound sales closing

Real estate or property sales

Negotiation



Compensation



$180 monthly base salary

3% commission per closed deal

40 hours per week

U.S. business hours

A description of your sales closing experience



Important

title: Figma Designer (Full-Time) – Social Media Carousel Templates
description:
We’re looking for a talented full-time Figma designer to help us build a large library of high-quality social media carousel templates.



These designs will be used by real estate professionals around the world to create engaging social media content quickly and easily.



You must be creative, detail-oriented, and very comfortable using Figma.



This is a long-term full-time role working directly with our team.



What You’ll Be Designing



You will be designing carousel templates for social media, such as:



• Instagram /
 carousels

• Educational carousels

• Marketing / promotional templates

• Infographic-style content

• Quote and tip graphics



These templates will later be used inside our platform to automatically generate social media content for users.



Strong visuals are critical because the image often determines whether people stop scrolling and engage with a post.



Example of the Work



You will create templates such as:



• “5 Tips for Selling Your Home”

• “Local Market Update”

• “Property Marketing Strategy”

• “First Home Buyer Tips”

• “Investor Insights”



Each design will include multiple slides such as:



Cover slide



Content slides



Summary slide



Call-to-action slide



Requirements



You must have strong experience with Figma.



Please only apply if you meet the following:



• Advanced Figma skills

• Experience designing social media graphics or carousel posts

• Strong layout and typography skills

• Ability to follow design systems and template structures

• Ability to work full-time and communicate regularly

• Good written English



Bonus if you also have experience with:



• Canva template design

• Social media content design

• Marketing graphics

• UI or template systems



What Success Looks Like



The ideal designer will:



• Create clean, modern social media designs

• Follow consistent layout systems

• Work efficiently to produce many templates

• Pay attention to spacing, hierarchy, and readability

• Design templates that look great on mobile screens



Working With Us



We are building a global content platform used by real estate professionals.



You’ll be helping create the visual template library that powers thousands of social media posts.



This is a long-term role with stable work.



Important – Read This



To make sure you actually read the job post, please start your application with the words:



“FIGMA CAROUSEL DESIGNER”



Applications that do not include this will not be reviewed.



Also include:



Your portfolio or Figma examples



Your years of Figma experience



Your favourite design style (minimal, modern, bold, etc.)



Salary



Please include your expected monthly salary for full-time work.



 
 with your application. DO NOT APPLY ONLINE



If you're a talented Figma designer who enjoys creating beautiful social media designs, we'd love to hear from you.

title: Sr. Technical Recruiting Sourcer
description:
Senior Technical Recruiting Sourcer / Sourcing Lead (Remote)



US Engineering & Aerospace Recruiting Firm





We are a 35-year U.S. executive search firm specializing in engineering and aerospace talent acquisition. Due to rapid growth with several clients, including a recently acquired aerospace company expanding its engineering teams, we are seeking a Senior Technical Recruiting Sourcer to help scale candidate sourcing efforts.







This is a remote virtual position working directly with the firm’s President.





The ideal candidate Must have experience sourcing technical talent and will also provide guidance and oversight to another Virtual Assistant supporting recruiting operations,

 Must be an expert with using Technologies - (This is not a Junior/Trainee role)





Key Responsibilities



Technical Candidate Sourcing





• Identify qualified engineering candidates using LinkedIn Recruiter Lite, LinkedIn, and other sourcing/Boolean tools (Must provide new ideas and be creative)



• Build targeted candidate lists using Boolean search techniques



• Send outreach messages to potential candidates



• Track responses and maintain organized pipelines in our CRM



• Conduct initial candidate qualification via messaging or





Sourcing Strategy



• Assist in developing sourcing strategies for multiple engineering searches



• Recommend improvements to candidate outreach and sourcing workflows



• Identify, implement and control new platforms and tools to locate technical talent





Team Coordination



• Provide guidance and task direction to one additional Virtual Assistant



• Help ensure sourcing activities and candidate tracking are properly executed



• Assist with organizing candidate pipelines and sourcing initiatives



Types of Roles You Will Source



Most searches involve highly technical roles such as:



• Systems Engineers



• Embedded Software Engineers



• Flight Controls / GNC Engineers



• Electrical Engineers



• Manufacturing Engineers



• Aerospace Engineers



• Robotics / Autonomy Engineers







Experience sourcing engineering or technical candidates is a MUST





Required Skills



Candidates should have experience with:



• LinkedIn sourcing and candidate outreach



• Boolean search techniques



• Technical recruiting or candidate sourcing



• Written English communication



• Spreadsheet / CRM organization



• Ability to work independently in a remote environment







Preferred experience:



• LinkedIn Recruiter or Recruiter Lite



• Aerospace, robotics, or engineering recruiting



• Apollo, Instantly, linkedhelper or  other outreach tools and be able to evaluate and make recommendations for better/different technology stacks









Weekly Performance Expectation





A successful sourcer should be able to generate approximately:



• 200–300 candidate profiles identified weekly



• 100+ outreach messages sent



• 15–25 candidate responses





Work Schedule



Remote position



Part-time or full-time depending on experience



NOTE:  MUST be able to provide examples/history of past success along with references.







This role may grow into a larger recruiting operations leadership role as our sourcing initiatives expand.

title: Legal Assistant
description:
We are a law firm in Southern California with an offshore team of legal assistants. We’re looking to add another key tea
mber to our ranks for an immediate start.



We believe in technology. We harness it to our advantage. Understanding how to manage, adopt, train, and utilize new technologies is an essential requirement for all members of our organization.



What’s the work?



• Legal tasks such as preparation of pleadings, legal documents, e-filing with attorney services, process service, calendaring hearings and deadlines/reminders, etc.

• Respond to client
s within 30 minutes.

• Correspond with clients, gathering information and scheduling meetings with our litigation attorneys.

• Attend recurring meetings such as litigation meetings, process meetings, paralegal training, etc.

• Follow up on pending tasks.

• Answer phone calls courteously and professionally; listen and understand the reason for the client’s call.

• Remind attorneys about deadlines and hearings.



What skills and experience do we want and need?



• Experience in litigation required (including preparation/drafting of pleadings, proofs of service, discovery, litigation timeline, and trial preparation).

• Experience in bankruptcy is a plus.

• Experience in lender law, unlawful detainers, replevin, collections is a plus.

• Ability to manage workload and work well under pressure.

• Ability to take responsibility for tasks assigned from beginning to completion.

• Proficient in Excel, Word, Outlook, Zoho; knowledge of legal billing systems (ProLaw) is a plus.

• Some College Degree or equivalent.

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

• Savvy with technology applications.

• Strong internet connection.



Qualities:



• Reliable

• Accountable/Honest

• Observant

• Punctual

• Proactive

• Teachable



Working hours:



• Monday – Friday: 40 hours per week

• Must be willing to work at U.S./PST time:

• 7:00 am PST through 4:00 pm PST (11:00 pm through 7:00 am/next day Manila time) with one hour break.

• Change of schedule is subject to negotiation.



IT Requirements:

• Own a laptop with at least Windows 10, Processor I5 Core-1235U 1.30 GHz, 16GB

• Must complete a speed test, meet system specification requirements, and meet laptop specification requirements. Additional
 with more instructions will be sent as part of the application process.



Next Steps: PLEASE READ THE INSTRUCTIONS CAREFULLY BELOW.



To apply, please copy the link below and paste it into your browser to complete the application form. You will need a Google account to upload the required application materials.



Application Form Link: 





Please ensure that you provide the following as part of your application:

1. Resume (PDF version)

2. Writing Samples

3. Voice recording: Tell us about something you have accomplished, handled, or organized in a prior employment

4. Link to your profile on 

5. Answer the questions below in the Google Form:

• Legal experience in litigation is a requirement.

• What are the names/descriptions of pleadings or legal documents you prepared in previous employment?

• Were these documents filed with the court?

• If so, were you personally responsible for submitting pleadings or legal documents for filing with the court?

• If yes, did you use an attorney service? How was the filing submitted?

• Have you prepared pleadings for Trial preparation? What were the pleadings?

• Have you ever prepared a Proof of Service? What is its purpose?

• Describe your experience working with state court, federal court, or bankruptcy court.

• Have you ever contacted a court and spoken with a court clerk? If yes, please describe the purpose of the call and the type of information you handled.

• Have you ever worked remotely for a U.S. company? If yes, what was the State and what were the U.S. hours?

• What is your availability to start employment (i.e., immediately, need 2 weeks, etc.)?

title: Conference & Events Coordinator
description:
Role Overview



The Conference & Events Coordinator provides operational and administrative support for Fortra Conferences and related events. This role is responsible for maintaining conference systems, supporting attendee and sponsor coordination, assisting with marketing workflows, and helping ensure event information is accurate and up to date across platforms.

 

This position works closely with the Marketing, Events, and Graphics teams and supports senior conference leadership with execution and coordination.



________________________________________

Key Responsibilities

Conference Systems & Data Management

• Maintain and update Airtable, Wrike, and Cvent for all conferences

• Add and update attendee, sponsor, and speaker records across systems

• Regularly audit attendee and sponsorship tabs to ensure accuracy and consistency

• Upload attendee lists to Wrike and shared drives; assist with internal and partner distribution

• Update Airtable following mail merges and registration updates

________________________________________

Sponsor & Attendee Coordination

• Register confirmed sponsors in Airtable, Wrike, and Cvent

• Assist with tracking sponsorships, registrations, and deliverables

• Support attendee communications and internal reporting needs

________________________________________

Marketing & Website Support

• Maintain and update conference websites with current information

• Coordinate with the graphics team on:

 o Conference graphics

 o Sponsor materials

 o Speaker bios and headshots

 o Swag and signage requests

• Submit graphic request forms for approved, straightforward items

________________________________________

Project & Administrative Support

• Create Wrike projects for confirmed conferences

• Update marketing agendas, Wrike tasks, Excel sheets, and conference agendas based on meeting notes and leadership direction

• Create calendar invites for new conferences, meetings, and events

________________________________________

Travel & Event Logistics Support

• Assist with coordinating travel and hotel logistics with partners’ assistants once conference attendance is confirmed

• Book travel for the marketing team when applicable

________________________________________

Ideal Candidate

• Highly organized and detail-oriented

• Comfortable working across multiple systems and tools

• Strong follow-through and ability to take direction

• Clear written and verbal communicator

• Interest in conferences, events, and marketing operations

________________________________________

Specific Requirements:



(a) Proficiency in the English language

(b) Must have reliable power and internet connection

(d) Work Pacific Time (Main Office is located in California)

(e) Must own a personal laptop (Windows or Mac) with a valid, fully licensed operating system. Minimum specifications: Windows 10 or macOS 12, Intel Core i5-1235U 1.30 GHz processor (or Apple M1/M2 equivalent), and at least 16GB of RAM.

(f) Must complete a speed test, meet system specification requirements, and meet laptop specification requirements.



Hours: 8:30 am—5:00 pm, PT, Monday through Friday

Salary: $800-$1000 a month plus $75 supplemental pay to use toward health insurance

________________________________________

Next Steps: 

Send us a link to a PDF of your resume and include a brief explanation of why you think you're the best fit for this position.



We look forward to seeing your application!

title: Real Estate Virtual Assistant
description:
REAL ESTATE VIRTUAL ASSISTANT

Keller Williams Real Estate



POSITION SUMMARY:

We are a high-performing real estate team under Keller Williams Real Estate, seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Executive Assistant to provide high-level administrative, operational, and client support.

The ideal candidate is highly organized, tech-savvy, and thrives in a fast-paced environment.

This role is essential to ensuring that the team operates efficiently—managing CRM systems, coordinating transactions, overseeing marketing initiatives, and supporting leadership with day-to-day operations.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

Administrative and Executive Support:

• Manage the team’s CRM, calendar,
s, appointments, and daily workflow to ensure maximum efficiency.

• Coordinate and schedule meetings, showings, and events; prepare supporting materials in advance.

• Prepare buyer and listing paperwork, contracts, and supporting documentation.

• Organize and maintain both digital and physical filing systems, including contracts, client records, and compliance documentation.

• Draft correspondence, manage communications, and act as a liaison between leadership, clients, agents, and vendors.

• Send and manage ZipForm and DocuSign documents for new and existing clients.



Transaction Coordination:

• Update Command/CRM pipelines when new clients enter the system and maintain accurate records throughout their journey.

• Assist with real estate transactions from contract to close, ensuring accuracy, compliance, and adherence to deadlines.

• Maintain regular communication with clients, lenders, title companies, and cooperating agents to ensure smooth closings.

• Prepare and distribute listing packets, buyer presentations, and related materials as needed.



Marketing and Operations:

• Coordinate marketing efforts for listings, including photography, signage, social media campaigns, and blog updates.

• Track key performance metrics and prepare regular reports on team productivity, pipeline status, and lead conversion.

• Support client appreciation events, travel logistics, and team gatherings.



Team and Lead Management:

• Manage new incoming leads, ensuring systems are in place for tracking and follow-up.

• Support onboarding and training of new agents and tea
mbers.

• Continuously improve internal systems and workflows to enhance team efficiency and client experience.



QUALIFICATIONS:

• Minimum of 2–3 years of administrative or operations experience within real estate.

• Strong organizational, time-management, and problem-solving skills.

• Proficiency with Google Workspace, KW Command, CRM systems, ZipForm, DocuSign, Canva, Etc.

• Strong written and verbal communication skills with a high level of professionalism.

• Ability to handle sensitive information with discretion.

• Self-starter with strong follow-up skills and the ability to anticipate needs before they arise.

• Tech-savvy with working knowledge of social media, Canva, and marketing coordination tools preferred.



To Apply, fill out the form below:

title: Digital PR Specialist - HARO Link Building
description:
About The Ad Firm



The Ad Firm is a US-based, performance-driven digital marketing agency recognized for delivering measurable ROI through transparent strategies and proven results since 2009. We partner with businesses across industries to grow visibility, traffic, and revenue through precision-built campaigns.



Job Description



We are seeking a results-driven Digital PR Specialist who’s passionate about digital marketing. This role focuses on securing meaningful online coverage, strengthening brand visibility, and shaping a strong public presence across digital media outlets. The position suits someone who enjoys outreach, story development, and building steady relationships with journalists and publishers.



Key Responsibilities



- HARO Outreach: Identify relevant queries and craft compelling, tailored pitches that align with journalists’ needs.

- Pitch Crafting: Write high-quality responses that are clear, concise, and engaging, increasing the chances of securing placements in top-tier publications.

- Relationship Building: Develop and maintain relationships with journalists, editors, and industry influencers to foster long-term collaboration.

- Campaign Management: Organize and track outreach efforts, measure success, and adjust strategies to meet KPIs.

- Collaboration: Work closely with SEO strategists and content teams to align outreach efforts with broader SEO goals.



Required Qualifications



- Experience: Minimum 2 years of experience in digital PR, HARO outreach, or similar link-building activities.

- Pitching Expertise: Proven track record of writing successful pitches that secure high-DR backlinks.

- Research Skills: Ability to identify relevant journalist requests and select opportunities that align with clients’ industries and niches.

- Relationship Management: Skilled at building and nurturing relationships with media contacts.

- Communication: Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

- Organizational Skills: Detail-oriented with strong project management abilities.

- Familiarity with tools like Ahrefs or Semrush and different AI tools

- Experience working with clients in diverse industries.

- Understanding of SEO best practices related to link building.



Why Join Us



- Work with a diverse range of clients and industries.

- Collaborative, fast-paced, and growth-focused agency culture.

- Competitive salary and performance-based bonuses.

- Professional development support and SEO tool access.

- Flexible remote work options.

description:

We’re expanding our team and seeking a Social Media Specialist to execute social media strategies for our clients in alignment with their business goals, encompassing content writing, scheduling, community management, and reporting for social channels. The position will own all content creation, asset creation, publishing, and coordinating with cross-functional tea

 marketing experience is a plus

title: US CPA Firm Senior Operations Associate
description:
The CPA Dude | U.S. CPA Firm



Operations Associate – Tax Operations is a core member of the tax operations team at The CPA Dude, a fast-growing U.S. CPA firm serving high-net-worth individuals and businesses with complex tax needs. This role supports the execution of tax workflows, client operations, and revenue processes, ensuring filings are completed accurately, deadlines are met, and clients receive a consistently high level of service.



This position is ideal for an operations-driven professional who thrives in structured environments, enjoys coordinating across teams, and takes ownership of processes from onboarding through billing and collection.



Required Experience: 1-2 years of professional experience in related accounting or tax field

Preferred Experience: 1 year at a US tax company or CPA Firm.



Working Hours: 10AM CST - 6PM CST

Location: Remote

Compensation: $2500 USD Salary a Month







Key Responsibilities



Tax & Client Operations



Manage client onboarding workflows and maintain accurate client records throughout the engagement lifecycle

Perform pre-review checks on tax returns to ensure completeness, identify missing documentation, and flag discrepancies prior to submission

Prepare and file tax extensions and support e-filing across individual and business returns

Organize, label, and maintain digital client files to ensure accessibility and audit readiness

Create, roll forward, and maintain workpapers with a focus on accuracy and process improvement

Coordinate directly with clients via
 and phone to request documents, clarify information, and resolve operational issues

Collaborate with tax preparers, bookkeepers, and internal teams to keep workflows on track and deadlines met

Manage calendars, task rosters, and follow-ups to support timely delivery of services

Escalate risks, delays, or client issues to management with clear documentation

Billing, Collections & Revenue Support



Support billing processes by tracking engagement status and ensuring invoices are issued accurately and on time

Monitor accounts receivable and follow up on outstanding invoices in a professional, client-focused manner

Communicate with clients regarding billing questions, payment status, and collection follow-ups

Coordinate with leadership to resolve billing discrepancies and collection issues

Identify upsell opportunities within existing accounts and escalate to leadership (bonus eligible)

Operations Standards



Serve as a liaison between clients, contractors, and internal teams to ensure timely and accurate service delivery

Maintain same-business-day response standards and contribute to a fast, accountable operations culture

Communicate clearly, professionally, and confidently in all written and verbal interactions





Results

1. Ensure high client satisfaction and retention through exceptional client service

2. Drive revenue growth through identifying and communicating upsell opportunities

3. Maintain accurate and complete client files for efficient tax preparation

4. Ensure timely filing of all client tax documents and extensions

5. Foster positive client relationships as a trusted advisor

6. Demonstrate a measurable impact on client retention rates

7. Successfully onboard new clients with a smooth transition process

8. Contribute to the firm's growth through process improvement and efficiency

9. Maintain a high level of client responsiveness (same day or within 24 hours)

10. Support our tax advisors in delivering comprehensive tax planning services











Hiring Process

Apply and record a 
 or 
 introducing yourself and explaining why you are a good fit for this position.



 that & your Resume to : 



Prescreen interview (30 minutes)

Technical test (take-home)

Panel/culture interview (60 minutes)

title: US CPA Tax Firm Client Service Coordinator
description:
We are looking for someone with one of the following:

- 2 years of US CPA Firm experience 



Pay:

Starting at $10hr 



Working Hours

Regular hours: Usually EST/PST/CST Business Hours 

Busy season hours: 10AM to 10 PM US Hours with weekend work



Mission



Own the first 90 days of new engagements from tax filing, tax planning, accounting, etc. Get every client fully onboarded and informed, all tax organizers and documents collected, and keep them informed so they feel guided, not neglected. Your success is measured by speed to “ready for work” and client happiness. 



Responsibilities

Meet with prospects and leads via video calls

Call prospects direct to schedule onboarding calls 

Assist in filling out organizers

Act as customer service rep 





Location

Remote position with dedicated and secure workspace required, free of distractions.



1. New Client Onboarding & Activation

- Receive warm handoff from sales with full notes and call recordings to understand the client

- Schedule and host onboarding / document calls to walk clients through the portal and process.  

- Track each client’s status from “Closed” to “All docs in, ready for tax work.”



2. Tax Organizer & Document Collection (Core Duty)

- For each tax season engagement, review the client’s prior?year return and known facts.  

- Pre?fill the tax organizer as much as possible before the client call (basic info, known entities, prior?year amounts where applicable) so you use call time to confirm, not type.  

- Send organizer + clear checklist of remaining items.  

- Schedule and run “Organizer & Docs” calls to complete missing sections live and ensure uploads happen.  

- Execute a follow?up cadence 
, text, calls) until all required information and documents are received.



3. Client Communication & Basic Support (Core Duty)

- Be the primary point of contact for routine questions about: timelines, portal, organizers, extensions, basic tax terms. Provide comfort calls when the client is lost and making sure clients don’t churn from lack of service

- Proactively send status updates so clients know what’s happening and what’s next.

- Triage and route technical / advice?level questions to CPAs and tax strategists with a clear summary.  



4. Retention & Relationship Management

- Run a 90?day success path for new concierge members (onboarding, implementation check?in coordination with the associate tax advisor, 60? and 90?day touchpoints). 

- Monitor “at?risk” signals (unresponsive, repeated confusion, missed meetings) and book save?the?relationship calls.  

- Promote and remind clients about our Skool community, live calls, and trainings; share recaps and key takeaways.

- Offer feedback check-ins with $7k ARR clients to gauge sentiment and offer to schedule meetings, follow up on deliverables, and general feedback to boost retention 



Software You'll Use:



ProConnect (Intuit) – tax return and extension prep

TaxDome – CRM and secure client communication

QuickBooks Online – for coordinating with bookkeepers

Google Workspace – Docs, Sheets, Drive, Gmail (we don't use Microsoft Office)

Go High Level





Hiring Process

Apply and record a Loom introducing yourself and explaining why you are a good fit for this position.



 that to : 



Prescreen interview (30 minutes)

Technical test (take-home)

Panel/culture interview (60 minutes)

title: Tax Advisory Associate (US Taxes)
description:
We are looking for someone with one of the following:



- US Tax Planning or Tax Strategy Experience 

- US CPA Firm experience as an associate with 3+ years of experience



Role



Tax Advisory Associate will be helping with our Tax Advisory Department help meet clients, schedule workflow for tax planning, run tax projections and compile tax plans. This position is great for entry or senior level tax preparers and associates looking to get a feel for providing value to clients on a strategic level.





REQUIREMENTS AND RESULTS







This role is somewhat of a hybrid role between a tax preparer and a junior level tax strategist. We understand such roles are uncommon - most focus on preparation or planning; however, we are willing to put in the training to make sure you have the knowledge and support to succeed. 







This role will be the main liaison between the tax advisory and compliance teams. 







Systems and Process Skills 



Set up Locators in Proconnect for new clients 

Run
tax projections minimum in the software based on i) actual docs provided by client, ii) hypothetical planning scenarios, and iii) and/or

Run 50-100 quarterly tax estimates or extension calculations minimum.

Analyze the difference between potential planning scenarios 

Have a basic understanding of bookkeeping on Quickbooks

Analyze a client’s P&L/BS for clarity and accuracy 





Technical Tax Skills & Knowledge 



Have a basic understanding of tax preparation and tax returns 

What returns/forms may need to filed 

Understand the filing scope of the client and what docs may be relevant for a tax return 

A desire to learn tax planning is crucial for this role 

Review entity structure and create an Organizational Chart

Learn the tax strategies offered to clients in their respective Tax Plan 

Understand the step-by-step implementation for each strategy

Understand Cost Segregation studies and own the process

Get estimates based on data needed from the client 

Act as the intermediary between us and our cost segregation partners 





Communication Skills & Organization 



Sit on strategy calls (approximately
) and document the items discussed 

Draft up to-do’s after the call 

Assist clients in tax strategy implementation

Provide step-by-step instructions and guidance 

Respond to client tax planning questions in a timely manner - ideally same day

24 business hours response time (3 days or less)

Research Skills - we live in a world powered by internet and AI, must have the ability to research items on their own before reaching out for help 

Chase clients and make sure they understand and implement the strategies discussed

Send looms (recorded videos) to help guide the client 

Schedule calls with client to make sure they are getting the number of calls needed 





CORE VALUES

Our core values are the heart and soul of this incredible company. The right person for this role will appreciate each of these values, personally subscribe to them, and understand why each is critical to having a great business. 







Communication 

Respond to others how you would want to be responded to. You wouldn’t want to wait a day for a response, nor do others. Having kind candor and giving the not so great news is expected and needed. Using your voice is important and we want you to voice your opinion. 

We use Slack – it is the go to for any communications

We value over-communication (more context the better)

We value speed in communication (be prompt in responding or provide context as to why you may be slow to respond)

Weekly Tea
etings

Ownership

Deadlines are always met and you are able to own all your relationships internally and externally. Your work product is something you and the client are proud of. If a client had to rate your service on a scale of 1-10, would they give you a 10? 

Work smarter not harder

We are growing fast and in order to achieve our mission, this role will require a lot of hard work until we build into a greater balance. However we are looking for an addition who can get us there and by working smarter by leveraging better technology, process, and out of the box thinking.





Software You'll Use:



ProConnect (Intuit) – tax return and extension prep

TaxDome – CRM and secure client communication

QuickBooks Online – for coordinating with bookkeepers

Google Workspace – Docs, Sheets, Drive, Gmail (we don't use Microsoft Office)

Go High Level





AVAILABILITY



8-6 PM for any US time zone (preference for PST but flexible), can be flexible for the right candidates

Able to work additional hours on weeknights and weekends. In addition, there will be peak busy season hours. 



Busy season hours: 10 AM to 10 PM CST/PST/EST with weekend work





REPORTS TO - Lead Tax Advisor (Supervisor)LOCATION - Fully Remote!





BENEFITS

PTO - 15 days

Sick Leave/Holidays

No BS corporate culture - we're modern, laid back, and straight to the point

Professional development opportunities and continuous learning

Collaborative team culture with awesome co-workers

Opportunity to grow with a fast-expanding firm

Digital-first approach with cutting-edge tax technology

Work alongside tax professionals focused on innovative tax strategies

Entrepreneurial environment that rewards initiative and fresh ideas









Hiring Process



Apply and record a Loom/Video introducing yourself and explaining why you are a good fit for this position.





 that to : 





Prescreen interview (30 minutes)

Technical test (take-home)

Panel/culture interview (60 minutes)

title: Tax Manager (US Taxes)
description:
We are looking for someone with one of the following:

- Philippines CPA with US CPA Firm experience as an tax associate or senior tax associate

- US CPA Firm experience as an associate with 5+ years of experience

- Philippines large CPA Firm experience with some US tax experience



Role

Tax Manager at The CPA Dude, a fast-growing tax firm serving high-net-worth individuals and businesses with complex tax profiles. In this role, you will be the backbone of our tax operations, ensuring smooth client management, document handling, and supporting the tax preparation process. As a critical member of our team, you'll help deliver tax transformations with comprehensive care, embodying our mission to become the Nike of tax firms.



Responsibilities

Prepare complex tax returns

Manage client onboarding processes and maintain ongoing client relationships with a focus on client success

Review tax returns to identify missing documents, flag discrepancies, and ensure completeness before submission

Organize, label, and track client files for team access and review with meticulous attention to detail

Create and roll forward workpapers, evaluating them for accuracy and improvement

Communicate directly with clients via
 and phone calls to clarify information or request documents

Proactively engage with clients to maintain relationships and identify retention risks

Collaborate with bookkeepers, assistants, and tax preparers to gather necessary information

Escalate issues appropriately to management when needed

Command presence with strong verbal and written communication skills

Respond to client inquiries within the same business day (our fast response culture)

Liaise between clients, contractors, and internal teams to ensure timely delivery of services



Results

Ensure high client satisfaction and retention through exceptional client service

Maintain accurate and complete client files for efficient tax preparation

Ensure timely filing of all client tax documents and extensions

Foster positive client relationships as a trusted advisor

Demonstrate a measurable impact on client retention rates

Successfully onboard new clients with a smooth transition process

Contribute to the firm's growth through process improvement and efficiency

Maintain a high level of client responsiveness (same day or within 24 hours)

Support our tax advisors in delivering comprehensive tax planning services



Requirements



1. 1-3 years of experience in U.S. tax operations and/or public accounting

2. Strong understanding of U.S. tax return structures (individual and business)

3. Experience with document management and preparation for tax filing

4. Proficiency with Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive, Gmail, pivot tables, filters, formulas)

5. Experience with U.S. tax software (e.g., ProConnect, QuickBooks Online, TaxDome)

6. Strong attention to detail and organizational skills

7. Excellent client-facing and call handling abilities - must be comfortable with video and phone calls

8. Critical thinking skills to determine what's missing or needs fixing

9. Research-first mindset – ability to leverage AI, internet tools, or tax guides before asking for help

10. Strong written and spoken English communication skills

11. Ability to work remotely with a high degree of autonomy

12. Killer project management and follow-up skills

13. Ability to adapt to new systems and technologies quickly

14. Comfortable working with 4 hours overlap in US hours

15. Ability to manage multiple tasks and prioritize workload while maintaining calm

16. A dedicated and secure workspace, free of distractions



Location

Remote position with dedicated and secure workspace required, free of distractions.



Compensation



Transparent compensation structure based on experience and performance



Get paid what you're worth - we reward based on performance, not billable hours



Bonus structure for identifying and closing upsell opportunities

Competitive salary with a clear growth path



$2000 a month 



Benefits



1. 100% remote work environment with the infrastructure to work from anywhere in the world

2. No BS corporate culture - we're modern, laid back, and straight to the point

3. Professional development opportunities and continuous learning

4. Collaborative team culture with awesome co-workers

5. Opportunity to grow with a fast-expanding firm

6. Digital-first approach with cutting-edge tax technology

7. Work alongside tax professionals focused on innovative tax strategies

8. Entrepreneurial environment that rewards initiative and fresh ideas



Core Values



1. Communication: Honest, kind candor with prompt responses. We value over-communication—more context is always better.

2. Ownership: Taking full responsibility for work and relationships. Deadlines are non-negotiable, and quality work is expected.

3. Work Smarter, Not Harder: Leveraging better technology, refining processes, and bringing fresh, creative thinking.



About Us



Our mission is to deliver tax transformations with comprehensive care by becoming the Nike of tax firms. The CPA Dude was created based on all the procedures that the founder didn't like about public accounting and made a firm based on output and not billable hours.



We are a modern, laid-back, and straight-to-the-point tax firm. While our name is approachable, our work demands precision and expertise. We offer tax compliance but are truly focused on consulting with Tax Advisory Services (TAS).



We serve high-net-worth individuals and businesses with complex tax profiles, specializing in tax planning and structuring for real estate investors, business owners, content creators, day traders, tech professionals, and high-earning 1099 contractors. Our focus is on maximizing tax planning strategies that make the biggest impact, including:



1. Entity & compensation optimization

2. Real estate industry strategies (Asset sales, Depreciation, 1031, Real estate professional, etc.)

3. Workhorse strategies (Augusta, Accountable plan, travel optimization, etc.)

4. Retirement optimization

5. And much more "nerdy tax stuff"



We're not just a tax firm—we're a forward-thinking team that values autonomy, clear communication, constant improvement, and mutual respect. We give our clients the chance to showcase real tax savings when we file their returns.



Software You'll Use:



ProConnect (Intuit) – tax return and extension prep

TaxDome – CRM and secure client communication

QuickBooks Online – for coordinating with bookkeepers

Google Workspace – Docs, Sheets, Drive, Gmail (we don't use Microsoft Office)

Go High Level



Working Hours

Regular hours: 8 Hours with 4 hours overlap in US time

Busy season hours: 10 AM to 10 PM PST with weekend work



Hiring Process

Apply and record a Loom introducing yourself and explaining why you are a good fit for this position.



 that to : 



Prescreen interview (30 minutes)

Technical test (take-home)

Panel/culture interview (60 minutes)

8. Entrepreneurial environment that rewards initiative and fresh ideas



Core Values



1. Communication: Honest, kind candor with prompt responses. We value over-communication—more context is always better.

2. Ownership: Taking full responsibility for work and relationships. Deadlines are non-negotiable, and quality work is expected.

3. Work Smarter, Not Harder: Leveraging better technology, refining processes, and bringing fresh, creative thinking.



About Us



Our mission is to deliver tax transformations with comprehensive care by becoming the Nike of tax firms. The CPA Dude was created based on all the procedures that the founder didn't like about public accounting and made a firm based on output and not billable hours.



We are a modern, laid back, and straight-to-the-point tax firm. While our name is approachable, our work demands precision and expertise. We offer tax compliance but are truly focused on consulting with Tax Advisory Services (TAS).



We serve high-net-worth individuals and businesses with complex tax profiles, specializing in tax planning and structuring for real estate investors, business owners, content creators, day traders, tech professionals, and high-earning 1099 contractors. Our focus is on maximizing tax planning strategies that make the biggest impact, including:



1. Entity & compensation optimization

2. Real estate industry strategies (Asset sales, Depreciation, 1031, 3.Real estate professional, etc.)

4. Workhorse strategies (Augusta, Accountable plan, travel optimization, etc.)

5. Retirement optimization

6. And much more "nerdy tax stuff"



We're not just a tax firm—we're a forward-thinking team that values autonomy, clear communication, constant improvement, and mutual respect. We give our clients the chance to showcase real tax savings when we file their returns.



Software You'll Use: 



ProConnect (Intuit) – tax return and extension prep

TaxDome – CRM and secure client communication

QuickBooks Online – for coordinating with bookkeepers

Google Workspace – Docs, Sheets, Drive, Gmail (we don't use Microsoft Office)

Go High Level



Working Hours

Regular hours: 10 AM to 6 PM CST

Busy season hours: 10 AM to 10 PM CST with weekend work



Hiring Process

Apply and record a Loom introducing yourself and explaining why you are a good fit for this position. 



 that to : 



Prescreen interview (30 minutes)

Technical test (take-home)

Panel/culture interview (60 minutes)

title: Real Estate SMS Lead Manager (Smarter Contact Required)
description:
We are a U.S. real estate investment company that acquires off-market properties through high-volume SMS marketing using Smarter Contact.



We send tens of thousands of texts per month and receive hundreds of responses from property owners.



Our acquisitions team purchases properties directly from motivated sellers.



We are hiring a Lead Manager to review incoming SMS conversations, qualify sellers, and ensure the best opportunities are immediately escalated to our acquisitions manager.



Speed, judgment, and organization are extremely important in this role.



IMPORTANT REQUIREMENT



This position requires prior experience working with real estate SMS lead generation.



You must have experience using:



• Smarter Contact or similar SMS platform

• Real estate wholesaling lead management

• Zillow or Redfin property research

• Google Sheets / Google Workspace



If you have never worked with real estate texting campaigns, please do not apply.



Responsibilities



Your job is to manage incoming seller conversations and identify the best opportunities.



Daily responsibilities include:



• Reviewing incoming seller responses from SMS campaigns

• Responding to sellers using scripts

• Asking qualifying questions about the property

• Looking up properties on Zillow to estimate value

• Categorizing leads as Hot / Warm / Cold

• Recording notes inside Google Sheets or CRM

• Flagging hot leads immediately for the acquisitions manager to call



The goal is simple:



Never miss a motivated seller.



Example of a Hot Lead



A lead is considered Hot if:



• Seller wants to sell soon

• Asking price is below Zillow estimate

• Property needs repairs

• Seller shows motivation



Hot leads must be flagged immediately so the acquisitions manager can call them right away.



Skills Required



Strong written English

Fast response time

Ability to manage many conversations simultaneously

High attention to detail

Ability to identify motivated sellers quickly



Compensation



$4.50–$5/hour depending on experience



Bonuses available for deals that close from leads you manage.



Opportunity to grow into a full-time lead manager role as we scale.



Application Questions



Please answer the following questions in your application.



Question 1



Zillow estimate shows $220,000.



Seller says they would sell for $140,000 because the house needs repairs.



Would you classify this as Hot, Warm, or Cold?



Explain your reasoning.



Question 2



A seller replies:



“Maybe I would sell depending on the offer.”



What three questions would you ask to determine if this is a real opportunity?



Question 3



You receive 120 seller responses in one hour.



How would you d
 which conversations to respond to first?



Explain your process.



Final Instruction (Important)



Start your application with the word DEALFLOW.



Then include:



• Your experience with Smarter Contact or SMS lead platforms

• Any experience working with real estate wholesalers or investors

• The number of seller conversations you typically handle per day



Please also include screenshots of SMS conversations you have handled with property sellers.



Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be reviewed.

title: Senior Architect
description:
Engagement Overview



Simour Design is seeking an experienced Architectural Contractor to support the development of technical drawings and code-compliant documentation for high-end medical and commercial design projects across the United States.



This is a remote independent contractor engagement. The contractor will provide architectural services on a project or ongoing engagement basis and retain discretion over the manner and timing of completing the services, while coordinating with the Simour team during an overlapping collaboration window.



For project coordination, we collaborate during a 3–6 PM PT window.



About Simour Design



Simour Design specializes in designing high-end medical interiors throughout the United States. Our work focuses on creating environments that are functional, brand-aligned, and compliant with U.S. codes and healthcare standards.



Scope of Services



The selected contractor will support architectural documentation and coordination for medical design projects. Services may include:



• Developing architectural and construction drawing packages

• Preparing code-compliant layouts aligned with ADA and U.S. building regulations

• Supporting design teams with technical documentation during project development

• Reviewing drawing sets prior to submission to ensure accuracy and completeness

• Coordinating with design consultants, architects, and project partners when required

• Assisting with material take-offs or technical evaluations as needed

• Supporting documentation required for permit submissions where applicable



Deliverables and timelines will be defined per project engagement.



Desired Experience



• Bachelor’s Degree in Architecture.

• 5+ years of commercial or healthcare design experience

• Experience working on projects that require U.S. building code compliance (ADA familiarity preferred)

• Strong portfolio of commercial or healthcare projects

• Proficiency with industry-standard design tools such as Revit, AutoCAD, SketchUp, and similar platforms

• Ability to collaborate with distributed teams and consultants



Application Requirements



We will keep all communication within this platform during the selection process. Please submit your application and the following items:



• Resume or CV

• Portfolio showcasing relevant commercial or healthcare projects

• Brief note describing your experience working on projects requiring U.S. codes or ADA compliance

• Your typical hourly or project-based rate

title: $8,000 From ONE Partnership - Need Crypto Network, Not Experience (Remote BD)
description:
Job Description:

We’re a DeFi protocol launching game-changing LP bond technology. We need Business Development partners who can introduce us to crypto launchpads, blockchain projects, and DeFi communities.





THE OPPORTUNITY:

Every successful introduction that leads to partnership earns you serious money. One good partnership could net you $5,000-10,000 in your first month.





COMPENSATION:

Monetize your network through three massive income streams: guaranteed ETH salary, instant token unlocks, and recurring commissions. We pay you to open doors you’ve already built, while we handle 100% of the closing.



Guaranteed Base Pay: You will receive $10/hour (capped at 10 hours/week),       for simply activating your network. 50% paid in Ethereum (ETH) for immediate cash and 50% in $GMI tokens, which are subject to an 18-month vesting schedule, building a long-term position in the $GMI mutli-chain ecosystem.



Performance Bonus: Every 5 successful partnership you close triggers an immediate unlock of 20% your vested tokens. This allows you to bypass the 18-month vesting period and access your earnings as soon as you deliver results.

Note: A "successful partnership" is defined by the project signing the LP Bonds integration agreement and going live on our marketplace.



Additional Commission: 

- 25% commission on ALL upgrade fees from your partnerships

- Level 2 upgrades: $40 upgrade fee, you get $10 each

- Level 3 upgrades: $80 upgrade fee, you get $20 each

- Level 4 upgrades: $160 upgrade fee, you get $40 each



-  $500 bonus when your partnerships hit 25 Level 4 bonds

-  $2,000 bonus at 100 Level 4 bonds

-  $5,000 bonus at 300 Level 4 bonds

-  Realistic month 1 earnings: $2,000-5,000

- Total 90-day potential: $20,000+



YOU HAVE:

- Existing connections in crypto (launchpads, DAOs, blockchain projects)

-
/
 relationships with project founders or marketing heads

- Understanding of DeFi/crypto space

- Hunger to earn based on results, not hours





YOU DON’T NEED:

- To close deals (I handle that)

- Any upfront investment

- Previous BD experience (connections matter more)

- Full-time commitment (work your own schedule)





THE ROLE:

Simply introduce me to decision-makers at crypto projects. I close the deals, you collect commissions. Every user from your partnerships who upgrades their bonds puts money in your pocket..

Example: ONE medium-sized launchpad partnership generating 100 L4 bonds = $8,000 for you.



Ready to turn your crypto connections into serious income?



Apply with:

- List 3-5 crypto projects/launchpads you have connections with

- Your
 handle

- Brief description of your crypto involvement



We need partners who see the opportunity and want to run with it. Start earning within days of your first successful introduction!

title: Senior AWS DevOps Engineer – AWS Cost Optimization & Kubernetes
description:
Job Description:

About the Role

We're a blockchain/DeFi company running production infrastructure that's bleeding money unnecessarily. Our AWS bill is sitting at $4,500/month – and we know it shouldn't be anywhere near that. We need someone who can come in, audit the whole setup, and actually fix it. Not just give us a report that sits in a folder.



We've already been through a few candidates who talked a big game but couldn't execute. We're done with that. If you're the type who needs handholding or can't work independently, this isn't for you.





What You'll Actually Do

- Audit our entire AWS infrastructure and identify exactly where we're wasting money (we expect significant savings)

- Optimize our Kubernetes clusters – right-size nodes, fix resource requests/limits, implement autoscaling that actually works

- Implement cost controls: Reserved Instances, Savings Plans, spot instances where appropriate, and cleanup of orphaned resources

- Set up monitoring and alerts so costs don't creep back up without us knowing

- Document everything you do so the team can maintain it





Hard Requirements (Non-Negotiable)

- 3+ years hands-on Kubernetes experience in production environments – EKS specifically preferred

- Deep AWS knowledge: EC2, EKS, RDS, S3, CloudWatch, Cost Explorer, Trusted Advisor

- Proven track record of actually reducing AWS costs (be ready to share numbers/percentages)

- Strong scripting skills (Python, Bash) for automation

- Infrastructure as Code: Terraform or CloudFormation (Terraform preferred)

- Self-starter who can take ownership and execute without constant oversight





!!!!CRITICAL: This Role Requires Surgical Precision

This is production infrastructure running live systems. Think of this like surgery – one wrong move can take everything down.

- NO cowboy moves. You don't delete, modify, or remove anything without a clear plan first.

- Every change must be planned out, documented, and confirmed with the CEO before execution.

- Extreme attention to detail. You double-check and triple-check before touching anything.

- If you're not 100% sure what something does, you ask first. You don't assume.

- Methodical execution only. We value careful and correct over fast and reckless.





Strong Plus

- Experience with blockchain/Web3 infrastructure

- Karpenter, Cluster Autoscaler, or KEDA experience

-  AWS certifications (Solutions Architect, DevOps Engineer)

- Experience with FinOps tools (Kubecost, CloudHealth, 
)





What We Need In Your Application

Don't send a generic application. We will ignore it. Include:

- Specific examples of AWS cost optimization you've done – what was the setup, what did you change, what % did you save?

- Your Kubernetes experience – cluster sizes you've managed, scaling strategies you've implemented

- Your availability and timezone (we need someone who can overlap with US business hours for some calls)





Compensation & Structure

- $20-25/hour based on experience and proven results

- 20 hours/week to start – potential for more hours for the right person

- Fully remote

- Initial project: Comprehensive audit + implementation plan with clear cost reduction targets





If you're someone who can actually deliver results and not just talk about them – we want to hear from you. Show us what you've done.

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer (DeFi and NFT Projects)
description:
About Energi

Energi (NRG) is a scalable and secure blockchain ecosystem, designed to power the next generation of Web3 applications, DeFi protocols, and NFT marketplaces. Built on a robust Proof of Stake (PoS) infrastructure, Energi combines Ethereum compatibility, on-chain governance, a self-funding treasury, and a Layer 2 masternode network to drive innovation in decentralized technology.

Our remote-first team spans the Americas, Europe, Africa, Asia, and Oceania, working together to push the boundaries of blockchain scalability and security. With a growing network of global contributors, we are committed to building a blockchain that is not only secure but also developer-friendly and highly scalable.

Energi is engineered to support millions of users, making it an ideal platform for high-performance dApps, NFT marketplaces, and decentralized finance solutions. Our ecosystem is designed for fast, cost-effective transactions with a focus on long-term sustainability.

By fostering collaboration, innovation, and a forward-thinking culture, Energi is redefining the Web3 landscape. Explore our vision and mission at 
.





WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU:

-Work flexible hours from anywhere as part of a globally distributed team driven by innovation and shared values.

-Directly shape the future of blockchain technology in NFT development.

-Enhance your skills and creativity with cutting-edge blockchain, smart contract, and Web3 technologies.

-Be part of an advanced blockchain ecosystem and contribute to building next-gen NFT platforms.

-Competitive pay structure in a 50/50 split of ETH and NRG tokens.



Front-End Role

JOB OVERVIEW:

We seek a Blockchain Full-Stack Engineer specializing in NFT platforms to drive the front-end development of our decentralized applications. You’ll create immersive Web3 experiences while integrating blockchain-based features like Metamask and smart contracts.



JOB RESPONSIBILITIES:

-Work closely with UX designers to create seamless NFT marketplaces and dApps.

-Develop interactive front-end features integrating Web3.js and smart contracts.

-Optimize UI performance and ensure mobile responsiveness.

-Maintain existing applications and introduce new blockchain features.



ESSENTIAL REQUIREMENTS:

-3+ years of experience in front-end development.

-Proficient in React, Gatsby, and Web3 frameworks.

-Experience integrating blockchain wallets like Metamask.

-Strong analytical skills and attention to detail.

-Fluent English communicator with a passion for blockchain.



NICE TO HAVE:

-Experience with Web3, Infura, and The Graph.

-Familiarity with NFT metadata standards and ERC-721/ERC-1155.

-SEO optimization skills.



Back-End Role

JOB OVERVIEW:

We’re looking for a Blockchain Full-Stack Engineer to build the backend for NFT applications. You’ll be responsible for designing, maintaining, and optimizing APIs that connect blockchain smart contracts with scalable Web3 applications.



JOB RESPONSIBILITIES:

-Develop secure APIs that interact with NFT smart contracts.

-Optimize backend infrastructure for high-traffic NFT transactions.

-Implement blockchain indexing solutions for NFT metadata retrieval.

-Write unit tests to ensure security and efficiency.



ESSENTIAL REQUIREMENTS:

-3+ years of back-end development experience.

-Proficiency in Golang, NodeJS, and GraphQL.

-Experience with blockchain data structures and decentralized storage.

-Passionate about blockchain innovation and fluent in English.



NICE TO HAVE:

-Knowledge of smart contract security best practices.

-Prior experience with NFT minting platforms.

-Remote work experience.



This role is not open to 3rd party organizations or agencies. Any candidates sent will be considered free of any charges



Energi is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, pregnancy, disability, age, veteran status, or other characteristics.

title: Virtual Assistant – Fluent English | Lead Follow-Up & Estimate Booking
description:
We are looking for a highly motivated Virtual Assistant with excellent English communication skills (100% fluent) to help manage leads and support our sales process for a construction and aluminum systems company.



Your main responsibility will be communicating with potential customers, booking estimates, following up on quotes, and helping turn inquiries into closed jobs.



This position requires professional spoken and written English, confidence speaking with customers, and strong organizational skills.



Responsibilities include:



• Responding to incoming leads and messages

• Booking appointments for estimates

• Following up with potential customers after quotes are sent

• Calling or messaging leads to move projects toward closing

• Updating CRM and keeping track of conversations

• Supporting the sales team with organized follow-ups



Requirements:



• Fluent / native-level English (very important)

• Comfortable speaking with customers professionally

• Experience in customer service, sales, or lead follow-up preferred

• Organized, reliable, and responsive

• Ability to work during U.S. business hours



Compensation:



• Hourly pay + commission opportunities for closed jobs

• Long-term opportunity for the right candidate

• Growth potential within the company



We are looking for someone professional, motivated, and confident speaking with customers who can help us convert leads into real projects.

title: Graphic Designer with Advanced Skill Set I Fully Remote
description:
Monday–Friday | 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM PHT

100% Remote | Open to Filipino Applicants Only



About the Role

We’re looking for a highly creative and detail-driven Graphic Designer who wants to work on diverse, exciting design projects for global brands.

In this role, you won’t be limited to just one type of design. You’ll create digital ads, branding systems, social media graphics, illustrations, and motion-ready assets, allowing you to showcase your creativity across multiple formats.

You’ll collaborate with a talented team of designers while working in an environment that values creative freedom, professional growth, and high-quality work.

If you’re passionate about great design, continuous improvement, and creative collaboration, we’d love to meet you.



What You’ll Work On

Design for Global Brands

Create design assets used by internationally recognized brands such as Netflix, Nike, and Princeton University.

Collaborate with Creative Teams

Work closely with project managers and fellow designers to deliver high-quality visual solutions.

Design Across Multiple Formats

You’ll work on projects including:

• Branding kits and identity systems

• Social media graphics and digital ads

• Packaging designs

• Web and marketing graphics

• Illustration and motion-ready assets

Contribute Creative Ideas

Take ownership of your projects, bring fresh ideas to the table, and continuously improve your craft.

Grow as a Creative Professional

Participate in skill-sharing, training, and collaboration with a community of talented designers.



What We’re Looking For

? A strong and versatile design portfolio

? Proficiency in Adobe Photoshop, Adobe Illustrator, and Adobe InDesign

? Experience with Adobe After Effects is a plus

? Strong English communication skills

? Excellent attention to detail

? Passion for visual storytelling and impactful design



Technical Requirements

• Reliable computer capable of running design software

• Stable internet connection

• Webcam (720p or higher)

• Working headset or microphone



Perks & Benefits

title: Virtual Accounting Assistant
description:
VIRTUAL ACCOUNTING ASSISTANT

Real Estate and Property Management | Part-Time | Remote | $5-$7/hr USD



We are a U.S.-based real estate and property management company looking for a reliable, detail-oriented Virtual Accounting Assistant to support our day-to-day financial operations. This is a part-time remote role with consistent, ongoing work. You will collaborate directly with our management team and CPA.



PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES:



Enter vendor bills and invoices into DoorLoop and QuickBooks Online accurately and on time

Categorize bank and credit card transactions according to the chart of accounts

Match receipts and supporting documentation to transactions

Monitor outstanding invoices and maintain organized vendor records

Prepare and organize financial data for review by management and our CPA

Maintain organized digital files of invoices, receipts, and financial documents

During slower periods: support tenant file management, vendor tracking, data entry, and general administrative tasks





REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS:



DoorLoop and QuickBooks Online experience is required. Please do not apply if you have not used both platforms.

Proficiency in Microsoft Excel for financial tracking and data organization

Prior experience in bookkeeping, accounts payable, or accounting support roles

Comfortable coordinating with a CPA and following structured accounting processes

Highly detail-oriented with a habit of flagging unclear transactions rather than guessing

Dependable, communicative, and able to work independently with minimal supervision





NICE TO HAVE:



Experience with property management accounting across multiple units or properties

Familiarity with U.S. real estate accounting practices





COMPENSATION AND DETAILS

Rate: $5-$7/hr USD

Type: Part-time, Remote

Client Location: United States



HOW TO APPLY:

Read carefully. Incomplete applications will not be considered.



Step 1: Record a short video (2 to 3 minutes) introducing yourself. In your video, tell us your name, your experience with DoorLoop and QuickBooks Online, and, so we know you read this entire posting, describe your favorite book and explain what lesson or idea from it you have actually applied in your own life. This does not need to be work-related.



Step 2: In your cover letter or message, start your first sentence with the phrase "I have used both DoorLoop and QuickBooks Online." Applications that do not begin this way will not be reviewed.



Step 3: Include your resume with relevant bookkeeping or accounting experience highlighted.

We review every application personally. 



Strong applicants will be invited to a short Zoom interview. We are looking for someone to grow with us long-term.
